calendar.matters:
ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Compliance With The Stipulation And Order
Off Calendar Apr-16-2013 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paula Canny for Kelvin Metcalf (Beneficiary). John Boessenecker for Alice Lane (Trustee). Paul Epstein for and with Wayne Metcalf.

Mr. Boessenecker informed the Court that they accepted an offer of $451,000.00 by Mr. Pochu Liang Kuo, and that the title company is requesting a court order confirming the sale, showing the Me. Boessenecker has the authority to sell the property and instructing them how the proceeds are to be distributed. The Court instructed Mr. Boessenecker to file a drop off ex parte petition, to prepare the order and to have it approved as to form. The parties were asked to step out of the court room to discuss the issue of removal of the appliances, door knobs, etc. Upon returning, Mr. Epstein informed the Court that they reached an agreement as follows: 1) Wayne will pay $5,000.00 to Kelvin out of escrow
. 2) Wayne is responsible for the property taxes, insurance, and utilities through March 15, 2013. Everything beyond that point will be shared between Kelvin and Wayne. Mr. Epstein also stated that this is a total release with respect to anything related to the property. Mr. Boessenecker is to include the agreement in the ex parte petition. Mr. Metcalf addressed the Court and expressed his opinion. Today's status hearing is off calendar.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Compliance With The Stipulation And Order
Off Calendar Mar-19-2013 Continued To Apr-16-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Issue Of Rental Value And Fees
Off Calendar Feb-21-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paula Canny and Kelton Burgess for and with Kelvin Metcalf (Beneficiary). John Boessenecker and Phyllis Berenson for Alice Lane (Trustee). Paul Epstein for Wayne Metcalf.

Mr. Epstein stated that his Client moved out of the property over the weekend, that items are still in the property (some to go to Salvation Army and some that his Client needs to remove), that his Client thought he needed to be out on the 16th and that he expects everything to be out within two weeks. Mr. Boessenecker argued that Wayne should be sanctioned by way of his objections being overruled, that any additional fees with regard to breach of agreement should be allocated to Wayne and that a Writ of Possession should be issued today. Mr. Boessenecker also stated that no money was set aside for preparing the property for sale because Wayne would not agree to it and that neither his Client nor Kelvin have keys. Ms. Canny stated that any costs/fees incurred arising from Wayne should be borne by Wayne and Wayne alone, asked to Court to issue a writ today and stated that she does not believe Wayne will be out by the 16th. Ms. Berenson explained services provided by the Trustee to support fees. The Parties noted and agreed that page 1 line 19 of the Stipulation and Order filed October 17, 2012 should read January 15, 2013. Status on compliance with the Stipulation and Order was set for March 19, 2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The matter was taken under submission for the Court to rule on fees to be paid out of escrow. The Court will issue a Writ of Possession. Mr. Epstein will facilitate getting keys to Mr. Boessenecker.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR The Issue Of Rental Value And Fees
Off Calendar Jan-16-2013 Continued To Feb-06-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Enforcing Settlement Agreement; For Possession And Sale Of Real Property; For Rent; For Award Of Trustee Fees; And For Award Of Attorney Fees [Probate Code Sec 17200; Ccp Sec 664.6]
Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Vacated

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Signing The Stipulation
Off Calendar Oct-17-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for and with Wayne Metcalf. John Boessenecker for Alice Lane. Paula Canny for and with Kelvin Metcalf.

Mr. Epstein and his Client agree to Mr. Boessenecker selecting a Real Estate Listing Agent as long as it is not Bob Evans and commissions are no more than 5%. There is some dipsute with regard to the additional terms of the settlement in reference to rental value and $6,000.00 being paid out of escrow from Wayne's share for repair to the property. Mr. Epstein stated that his Client wants the Court to review Ms. Berenson's and Ms. Canny's fees and determine reasonableness, and that they were objecting to Ms. Lanes fees. The Court provided the Parties with a copy of the minutes from the October 3, 2012 hearing listing a total of 12 items/terms. The Parties agreed to Wayne vacating the property by January 3, 2013, to issuance of a writ of possession, and to Wayne paying property taxes and insurance out of escrow for that period. The issue of fees is to be addressed on January 16, 2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Counsel can submit letters from a Realtor or declarations for the Court to determine reasonableness of rental value for the period of April 1, 2011 through December 31, 2012 and any additional information to support fees. Mr. Epstein's Client agreed to Ms. Canny's fees going up to $10,000.00. Counsel stepped into chambers to prepare a stipulation for entry of judgment pursuant to CCP 664.6 reserving the issue of fees and rental value which is to be signed by all Parties before they leave court today. Counsel stepped out of the courtroom to review the stipulation with their Clients. The stipulation was presented to Judge Wiss for final review. All Parties signed the stipulation. Endorsed copies of the stipulation was provided to all Parties. The Court encouraged Wayne to begin the process of sorting through his belongings and vacating the property timely. The hearing date of January 9, 2013 was vacated at Counsel's request.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition For Order Enforcing Settlement Agreement; For Possession And Sale Of Real Property; For Rent; For Award Of Trustee Fees; And For Award Of Attorney Fees [Probate Code Sec 17200; Ccp Sec 664.6]
Off Calendar Oct-03-2012 Continued To Jan-09-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for Wayne Metcalf (Respondent). Paula Canny for and with Kelvin Metcalf. John Boessenecker for Alice Lane (Trustee).

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions. The Court questioned why Wayne Metcalf has not moved for 1 1/2 year and the reason for an additional 6 months. Issues with regard to the Trustee's standing, attorney's fees, order to issue a writ of possession and entering into a listing agreement were addressed. All parties confer outside of court and returned to court with stipulation on additional terms. Additional terms are as follows:

1) Rent of $1,000.00 per month starting from 4/1/11 through 12/31/12 will be paid by Wayne to Kelvin out of Wayne's share from escrow.
2) Wayne will vacate the house by 1/3/13 midnight.
3) Kelvin will remove his personal property from the house by 12/15/12 midnight.
4) Wayne must remove all personal property by 1/3/13 midnight.
5) Writ of Possession will be issue to Kelvin for propose of effectuating the settlement agreement.
6) Mr. Boessenecker has sole authority to select the listing agent.
7) $7,500.00 will be paid to Paula Canny from Wayne's share from escrow.
8) $3,227.50 will be paid to John Boessenecker from Wayne's share from escrow.
9) Up to $6,000.00 from Wayne share out of escrow may be used to get the house ready.
10) $10,000.00 trustee fees for Alice Lane to be paid from escrow off the top.
11) $16,237.50 will be paid to Phyliss Berenson from escrow.
12) $19,839.00 will be paid to John Boessenecker from escrow.
All other terms of earlier settlement agreement remain in effect.

Mr. Epstein was unable to reach his client by phone during a recess to confirm the stipulation. The Court set Status on Signing the Stipulation on October 17, 2012 at 9:00 a.m. and the Petition for Order enforcing Settlement Agreement is continued to January 9, 2013 at 9:00 a.m. Mr. Epstein will prepare the order.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Ella Yip. Bailiff: Deputy Chien.

Petition To Compel Distribution Of Trust Property; For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And For Removal Of Trustee.
Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Advanced From Aug-23-10 To Aug-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition To Compel Distribution Of Trust Property; For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And For Removal Of Trustee.
Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Directing Sale Of Trust Real Property
Off Calendar Aug-13-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with Alice Lane. Paul Epstien for and with Wayne Metcalf. Paula Canny for and with Calvin Metcalf.

The Court stated that it saw a connection between todays Petition for Order Directing Sale of Trust Real Property filed by Mr. Boessenecker and the Petition to Compel Distribution of Trust Property; for Damages and Breach of Fiduciary Duty: and for Removal of Trustee filed by Mr. Epstien which was set for hearing on August 23, 2010. Therefore, the Court advanced the Mr. Epstein's petition to today to discuss posture and status of the matter. Discussions were held with regard to the length of time it would take to try these matters, whether the issue of breach of duty should be heard first, whether the matter should be referred out to the department of the Presiding Judge for trial purposes and the desire to conduct discovery. Both matters/petitions were referred to the department of the Presiding Judge (206) to be set for trial, (order for the same signed in open court). The Motions in Limine filed by Mr. Epstien are to be ruled on by the trial Judge. It is the Court's opinion that the trial should be set soon and that the issues that give rise to these two petitions have gone on far too long.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Jacqueline Alameda.

Petition To Compel Distribution Of Trust Property; For Damages For Breach Of Fiduciary Duty; And For Removal Of Trustee.
Off Calendar Jul-21-2010 Continued To Aug-23-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

Petition For Order Directing Sale Of Trust Real Property
Off Calendar Jun-07-2010 Continued To Aug-13-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstien for and with Wayne Metcalf. John Boessenecker for Alice Lane (Trustee). Paula Canny for Calvin Metcalf.

Mr. Boessenecker stated that the issue was with Wayne residing in the property since 1998, that they attended mediation but that they did not settle, and asked that the Court grant the petition requesting that Wayne not interfere with the sale. Mr. Epstein stated that he filed a petition to distribute the Trust today, that his Client and his Client's Brother asked the Trustee to distribute the property and that she has not done so. Ms. Canny said that she filed a Notice of Joinder in the petition to sell the property, that her Client has been excluded from the house, that the only way for his Client to get what the Grandma intended for him was to sell the property and distribute the proceeds. The matter was continued August 13, 2010 to 2010 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Harris.

Petition For Order Directing Sale Of Trust Real Property
Off Calendar Mar-01-2010 Continued To Jun-07-10 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Paul Epstein for Wayne Metcalf. Justin Goodwin for Kelvin Metcalf.

Mr. Epstein stated that he previously objected to Judge Dearman, that the Commissioner previously sat in on that day, and that he was ok with Judge Dearman sitting in today as long as he wasn't going to rule on any susbstantive matter. The Court confirmed that the Commissioner was not available today. Mr. Epstein stated that the parties mediated, that they were going back to Mr. Wolff on a fee basis, and that they had agreed to some tasks that needed to be completed. Mr. Goodwin stated that this was a general appearance by his office and that they joined in on the petition filed by Alice Lane to save fees. The matter was continued to June 7, 2010 for further status on the mediation.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.

Petition For Order Directing Sale Of Trust Real Property
Off Calendar Oct-06-2009 Continued To Dec-07-09 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard before Commissioner Kaplan due to a conflict with Judge Dearman.

Appearances: John Boessenecker for and with Alice Lane (Trustee). Paul Epstein for Wayne Metcalf.

The parties stipulated to the Commissioner sitting as a Judge Pro Tem as set forth on the record. Mr. Epstein stated that his client objected to Judge Dearman hearing this matter because Judge Dearman's Son is the Real Estate Agent. Mr. Boessenecker stated that they wanted to attend the Court's Pro Bono Mediation program, that the other party involved (Kelvin Metcalf) was not present, and that they wanted him ordered to attend the mediation that way everyone entitled to a stake in the matter will attend. Mr. Epstein stated that Kelvin is an interested person, that he should be ordered to attend the mediation, and that there is a stipulation that the property will not be listed at least until the mediation is completed as set forth on the record. The Court stated that if mediation fails it will go forward with the petition, that the matter is referred to Pro Bono Mediation which is to be completed by November 30, 2009, and set status for the mediation for December 7, 2009. In addition, the Court ordered counsel to contact the Examiner by the close of business on December 1, 2009 to advise him/her if the matter was successfully mediated.

Judge Pro Tem: Sue Kaplan. Reporter: Arlene Howell, CSR#2542. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy J. Gomez.
